shortAnswer: Platelets (PLT) are blood cells responsible for clotting. Low platelet counts can increase bleeding risk, while high counts may indicate inflammation or other conditions. Discuss abnormal results with your doctor.
metaDescription: Platelets (PLT) high or low? Normal ranges, causes, and what abnormal platelet counts mean — explained simply.
doctorQuestions: Does my platelet count pose an increased bleeding risk?
Is any of my medications affecting platelets?
When should I have the value rechecked?
